channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18834] Lustre can return negative stats value
item.description:
Looking at stats from obdfilter:

obdfilter.testfs-OST0000.stats=
snapshot_time             1742378286.332900343 secs.nsecs
start_time                1742228736.573307453 secs.nsecs
elapsed_time              149549.759592890 secs.nsecs
read_bytes                3469178 samples [bytes] 1048576 1048576 3637696790528 3814401549824688128
write_bytes               186442470 samples [bytes] 1001 16777216 3092142771667945 4612433686335277585
read                      3469178 samples [usecs] 623 16467 15904108552 85258323792650
write                     186442470 samples [usecs] 36 149845092510 -823495890607571055 4426400394686790401
punch                     562 samples [usecs] 10 48020 5760464 159896287662
sync                      719 samples [usecs] 0 76522 930977 13284701619
create                    11 samples [usecs] 2 30809 69396 1415757344
statfs                    58418 samples [usecs] 0 756 191916 2548952
get_info                  2 samples [usecs] 294 2488 2782 6276580
set_info                  48 samples [usecs] 5 15 562 6948

The following stat reports a negative value:

write                     186442470 samples [usecs] 36 149845092510 -823495890607571055 4426400394686790401

I suspect that's an overflow issue since negative value is not expected here.
item.summary: Lustre can return negative stats value

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58566/
Subject: LU-18834 obdclass: check overflow when doing sum in stats
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 2fdb3f6c9654c41a48aec6ece600d15a744ce5e8

"Emoly Liu <emoly@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58566
Subject: LU-18834 obdclass: check overflow when doing sum in stats
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 27eb648f1c2bdb5b724661f482e752558e4d8b87

It isn't clear that we would know if sumsq would overflow or not? It would just be a random 64-bit value at that point.

I think printing the values as positive is likely correct, since I don't think there are any stats which are negative values. However, the real question is whether some write RPC actually took 2 days to complete, or if there is some other bug?

It probably also makes sense to put "overflow detection" on these counters, like:

        new = old + incr;
        if (unlikely(new < old))
                new = LONG_MAX;

or similar, so that it doesn't overflow.

adilger ,

Hmm, but why sumsquare didn't overflow, it should be much bigger than sum? I checked the code and didn't find anything special to these values, defined in __s64 type and printed in "%lld".

So, will we change s64 to u64 to avoid the negative value?

It looks like the Maximum completion time for some of the write operations are reporting an unreasonably large value (149845092510 usec = 1.7 days), so either there is an overflow with the counter for a single operation, or there was some kind of problem that was causing the thread to be blocked for this length of time before it could finish. It would be worthwhile to check the server console to see if it reported stuck server threads and dumped stack traces.

Doing the math 186442470 writes * max 149845092510 usec/write = -8955998982476203532 approximately matches the reported sum.
